channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tailscale secures $160 million for its WireGuard-based VPN development
item.description: Virtual private network (VPN) technology has been a cornerstone of the networking landscape for decades, beginning first with the IPsec protocol then getting a bit easier with SSL-VPN technology. But little has changed with the technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft extends support for key Windows tool - but probably not the one you'd expect
item.description: Microsoft has taken the bold decision to postpone the removal of WSUS driver synchronization amid customer feedback.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Humanoid robot breakdances its way into history
item.description: Atlas robot by Boston Dynamics amazes tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson with its breakdancing moves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prices have leaked for the Google Pixel 10 series – as well as several generations of the Pro Fold
item.description: Your next Pixel phone could cost either more or less than the current models, depending on which you choose.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump administration disbands crypto regulations enforcement unit
item.description: The Trump administration disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team (NCET) according to a Monday memo issued by the Department of Justice (DOJ) outlining their efforts to remove regulatory framework for the growing digital finance sector.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IBM z17 mainframe gains AI accelerator, massive workload support
item.description: The Big Iron evolution continues. IBM has rolled out the latest iteration of its mainframe, replete with AI technology designed to take data-intensive application support well into the future.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok extension, potential deal may violate law: Top Senate Democrat
item.description: The top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence Committee warned Monday that President Trump’s additional extension for TikTok’s parent company to divest from the app, as well as a potential divestment deal on the table, may violate the law. In a letter to Trump, Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases
item.description: The White House says a new federal AI policy rejects the Biden administration's “risk-averse” approach, but one former official sees it as a “smart evolution." The post Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'demonstrably false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade adviser Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron" in their latest spat over the administration's new import taxes. "Navarro is truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Decision-making 101: How to get consensus right
item.description: IT leaders have five ways to make decisions. Their decision-making can be (1) Authoritarian; (2) Consultative; (3) through Delegation; (4) by submitting the alternatives to a Vote; or (5) by bringing the group to Consensus.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Polar Space Tourists Splash Down in Pacific After 3-Day Journey - News Article
item.description: Polar space tourists return to Earth after a 3-day journey aboard a SpaceX Dragon capsule, marking the first human spaceflight to circle the globe above the poles and the first Pacific splashdown for a space crew in 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DARPA backs multiple quantum paths in benchmarking initiative
item.description: The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency has selected 15 quantum computing companies for its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative, to see if utility-scale quantum computers can be built by 2033.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tariff war throws building of data centers into disarray
item.description: Enterprise IT leaders are facing a double-whammy of uncertainties complicating their data center building decisions: The ever-changing realities of genAI strategies, and the back-and-forth nature of the current tariff wars pushed by the United States. “This is obviously a fluid situation.
item.dc:creator: Network World
